prompt:
You are creating training-grade image descriptions for a LoRA or vision-language fine-tuning dataset.
The attached image is the primary source of truth.
Use the supplied OCR/context only when it clearly matches what is plausible in the image.
Do not hallucinate unreadable numbers, indicators, labels, or trading claims.
Write all natural-language fields in English.
Return JSON only. No markdown. No code fences.

{
  "short_caption": "8-20 words",
  "visual_summary": "1-3 sentences, image-grounded only",
  "context_augmented_summary": "1-4 sentences, may use matching OCR/context",
  "key_visual_elements": ["element_1", "element_2"],
  "limitations": ["unclear_text", "cropped_axis"],
  "visible_text": "short literal transcription of the most legible visible text",
  "training_tags": ["chart", "candlesticks", "price_scale"],
  "confidence": "high|medium|low"
}

Focus on what makes the image useful for training:
- visible chart structure, panels, axes, legend areas, candles, lines, annotations, arrows, highlighted zones
- visible symbol, timeframe, venue, labels, indicators, performance metrics, notes
- whether the crop looks like a trading chart, a performance panel, or another market-related figure
- for visible_text, copy one short contiguous snippet exactly as seen; do not join separate fragments with ellipses
- mention uncertainty explicitly in limitations when text or details are unreadable
